what_it_does: Static HTML/CSS design mockup of the desktop 'Canvas — empty / first-run' view (A1) for the operating surface, rendering the top bar, left node palette, empty canvas with a 'start here' affordance, thin activity strip, and right inspector panel in their empty states.
observations: Single-file HTML5 mockup with embedded CSS and no JavaScript.
Extensive header comments map the view to feature IDs (EVT-now, CAN-compose, NODE-types) and code references.
Implements a grid layout with top, rail, canvas, foot, panel, and a mobile tabbar.
Left rail lists all 16 node types grouped into two kinds: process (11) and content (5).
Empty canvas centers a 'start here' block offering two start moves: compose from the palette or open the real demo.
Footer activity strip reports presence = empty / nothing running yet with a hollow dot.
Right inspector panel shows an empty-state 'Nothing selected' and tabs for Inspector, Inbox, and RHM; no inbox badge is shown.
Uses CSS custom properties from an external design-system.css and data-ui-ref attributes to tag UI regions.
standouts: The file doubles as a specification document: comments explicitly forbid fictional UI elements such as invented templates, wizards, or fake activity.
Palette content is grounded to the node registry (16 self-registering types) rather than presented as a hand-maintained list.
Empty-state honesty is treated as a first-class design principle, with every omitted element justified by the absence of nodes or running events.
conventions: Flat, utility-style class names and CSS custom-property theming.
data-ui-ref attributes used to tag regions for cross-referencing with code or documentation.
Self-contained single-file mockup with inline styles.
Semantic-ish HTML structure using header, nav, main, footer, and aside.
Breaks typical mockup convention by embedding large amounts of design rationale and anti-fiction rules in HTML comments.
concerns: The run button is disabled but other 'quiet' toolbar buttons are only dimmed, which may be ambiguous without aria-disabled states.
Icon-only buttons and controls lack visible alt text or ARIA labels in this file.
The node list is hard-coded in HTML, so the mockup may drift from the actual registry if not regenerated.
The mobile tabbar is present but its desktop hiding depends on external design-system.css, making standalone behavior incomplete.
Some data-ui-ref values reuse paths like ui://canvas/inspector and ui://canvas/inbox for different elements, which could confuse semantic mapping.
notes: A meticulously annotated empty-state mockup that treats the first-run canvas as a verifiable spec, not just a picture.
questions: Are the 16 node names and counts (process 11, content 5) current with the actual registry at nodes/*.py?
Is 'focus' the real default RHM mode for first-run, or should the presence dial show a different posture?
Does the 'Open the demo' card load the codebase Q&A chain from a specific graph file or URL?
Are the quiet toolbar buttons intended to remain focusable/clickable despite their dimmed appearance?
Is the mobile tabbar hidden by design-system.css media queries, and does it duplicate the right-panel tabs?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file's stated purpose is to render the empty/first-run desktop canvas with honest empty states, and the markup and comments consistently do that.
apparent_themes: Single source of truth for node taxonomy
intention_signals: It is trying to define the first-run experience as a truthful, minimal invitation to compose, using the empty canvas itself as the onboarding surface rather than an overlay tour, and anchoring every visible element to an actual backend capability.
novelty: The extensive 'EMPTY-STATE HONESTY' commentary that explicitly forbids fictional UI elements and maps each affordance to a concrete feature ID or code reference, turning a visual mockup into a verifiable design contract.
contribution: A concrete, annotated empty-state layout and palette taxonomy that a fused system can reuse as the canonical first-run canvas structure, with clear data-ui-ref hooks and explicit rules about what must not be fabricated when no nodes or events exist.
summary_for_embedding: Static HTML/CSS mockup of the desktop empty/first-run canvas view with top bar, 16-node palette, honest empty-stage affordance, activity strip, and empty inspector panel.
intention_for_embedding: To specify the initial user-facing canvas state and the boundaries of permissible empty-state content before any nodes or events exist.
